subject: Anthropology
title: Marriage as Alliance
description: A short lesson to help you apply Marriage as Alliance.

hook.text: Marriage isn't just about love. For most of history, it was a treaty between groups, creating alliances and peace.

buildup.text: Anthropologists see marriage as a social institution that creates alliances between kin groups, not just individuals.

discovery.text: Levi-Strauss argued that exchanging women between groups creates bonds of reciprocity that hold societies together.

twist.text: Love-based marriage is historically rare. Arranged marriage for political and economic gain was the global norm.

climax.text: Marriage as alliance reveals that weddings are politics. Who you marry shapes power, property, and social networks.

punchline.text: Marriage ties groups together, not just people.

quiz.question: How did Levi-Strauss view marriage?
options: As exchange that creates bonds of reciprocity between groups
